name: business-optimization
title: Business Process Optimization
description: Adapts the harness to produce process-improvement business cases — current-state diagnoses, target-state designs, savings/benefit estimates — where the failure mode is an unsourced baseline, an impact figure that does not add up, or an improvement claim asserted without evidence, not a failing build.

stages.label: Frame
stages.purpose: Define the problem statement, the scope of the process under review, and the baseline metrics (current-state, dated and sourced) and constraints.
stages.output: Scope note: problem, process boundaries, baseline metrics

stages.label: Diagnose
stages.purpose: Establish root cause, lay out the options, and define the target state before any impact number is written. The plan the draft must follow.

stages.label: Draft
stages.purpose: Write the business case: current state, proposed change, reconciled impact figures, risks — each impact figure summing to its headline, each improvement claim sourced.

stages.label: Review breadth
stages.purpose: Scan every section for completeness and internal consistency; flag missing sections, orphan figures, unsourced claims.

stages.label: Review depth
stages.purpose: Run the gate verdicts: baseline integrity, impact reconciliation, claim substantiation, change feasibility & risk, ROI / mandate fit.

stages.label: Feedback
stages.purpose: Evaluate reviewer challenges, reconcile the recommendation, finalize the implementation risk view.

gates.name: Baseline Integrity
gates.question: Are the current-state metrics sourced and dated (system of record, time study, finance report) — none invented or stale?

gates.name: Impact Reconciliation
gates.question: Do the savings/benefit components sum to the headline impact figure, with no double-counting or unexplained gap?

gates.name: Claim Substantiation
gates.question: Is each improvement claim (percentage, superlative, absolute) sourced to evidence rather than asserted?

gates.name: Change Feasibility & Risk
gates.question: Are the implementation risks identified and specific, each paired with a concrete mitigation (not boilerplate)?

gates.name: ROI / Mandate Fit
gates.question: Does the recommendation's payback and ROI match the stated mandate, and are reusable analysis components factored out rather than re-derived?
